name: Excessive SMTP Connections from Single Source (Potential Spam/Compromise)
description: Detects an unusually high number of SMTP connections from the same source IP within a short timeframe, indicating email scanning, spamming, mass phishing, or a compromised host sending malicious email.

parameters.name: Timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of one of the SMTP connection events (e.g., the most recent one).

parameters.name: SourceIp
parameters.description: The IP address of the client initiating the SMTP connection.

parameters.name: DestinationIp
parameters.description: The IP address of the receiving mail server.

parameters.name: DestinationPort
parameters.description: The destination port (expected 25, 465, or 587 for SMTP).

parameters.name: User
parameters.description: The authenticated user associated with the SMTP traffic, if available.

parameters.name: Subject
parameters.description: The email subject line, if available from mail server logs.

parameters.name: From
parameters.description: The sender's email address, if available from mail server logs.

parameters.name: To
parameters.description: The recipient's email address, if available from mail server logs.

parameters.name: EventID
parameters.description: The specific Event ID from the log source, if applicable.

parameters.name: RuleName
parameters.description: The name of the Sigma rule that triggered the event, if applicable.

parameters.name: SIEM_COUNT_OF_EVENTS
parameters.description: The aggregated count of SMTP connections by the SIEM (e.g., 75).

parameters.name: SIEM_TIMEFRAME
parameters.description: The time window over which the events were counted (e.g., '1 minute').

parameters.name: SIEM_FIRST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the first event in the correlated group.

parameters.name: SIEM_LAST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the last event in the correlated group.
